steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you say when you see your friend in the morning?
questions.hint: We say this when we meet someone.

questions.question_text: When do we say 'Goodbye'?
questions.hint: We say it at the end, not at the start.

questions.question_text: What can you say to greet your teacher?
questions.hint: Think about the time when school starts.

items.definition: A greeting word
A casual greeting
To reply to a question
To hear attentively
A greeting when you meet someone
A friendly way to say hello
What you say when someone asks a question
Use your ears to hear carefully

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👋

steps.title: Meet and Greet
steps.text: Emma sees Tom at school. She says, 'Hello, Tom!' He says, 'Hi, Emma!' The teacher asks a question. They listen carefully. Tom answers first.
steps.scene_description: two children greeting each other at school with teacher in background

questions.question: Who says 'Hello, Tom'?
What do the children do when the teacher asks a question?
Who answers the teacher first?

steps.explanation: We use 'Who is' to ask about a person's name or identity.

items.explanation: Use 'is' with he, she, or it
Add 's' to verbs with he, she, or it
Use plural 's' with numbers greater than one
Correct! This is the right way to ask.

items.question: Who ___ your friend?
items.explanation: Use 'is' when asking about one person

items.question: Who ___ the teacher?
items.explanation: Use 'is' when asking about one person

items.question: ___ is he?
items.explanation: Use 'Who' to ask about a person's identity

steps.audio_text: Good morning, class! My name is Miss Brown. Please listen carefully. When I say hello, you answer 'Hi, Miss Brown!' Let's try it together. Hello, everyone!

questions.question: What is the teacher's name?
What should the children say when the teacher says hello?
What does the teacher want the children to do?

steps.prompt: Practice saying these sentences. Listen and repeat!

prompts.prompt: Say: Hello! My name is Emma.
Say: I listen to my teacher.

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet someone?
Who ___ your teacher?
Which word means to use your ears carefully?
True or False: 'Hi' and 'Hello' are both greetings.
When the teacher asks a question, you ___ .

steps.instruction: Let's meet some new friends! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet a new friend?
questions.hint: We say this when we first see someone.

questions.question_text: How many new friends are in our story today?
questions.hint: Count the children in the picture.

questions.question_text: What are our new friends' names?
questions.hint: Listen carefully to their names in the story.

items.definition: Used to refer to two or more people
What someone is called
Not old or not known before
Having existed for a long time

items.hint: Not new, had it a long time

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👥

steps.title: New Friends at School
steps.text: Ben and Kim are new in our class. They are friends. Ben says, 'My name is Ben.' Kim says, 'My name is Kim.' They are happy. Our old friends say hello!
steps.scene_description: Two young children smiling and waving in a bright classroom with other children in background

questions.question: Who are new in the class?
Are Ben and Kim friends?
What do the old friends say?

steps.rule_pattern: ___ is my friend. ___ are my friends.
steps.explanation: Use 'He' or 'She' for one friend, and 'They' for two or more friends.

items.question: ___ are new students.
items.explanation: Use 'They' for two or more people

steps.audio_text: Hello, children! Today we have two new friends in our class. They are Ben and Kim. Ben says, 'My name is Ben.' Kim says, 'My name is Kim.' Let's say hello to our new friends!

questions.question: How many new friends are in the class?
What are the new friends' names?
Are Ben and Kim old students or new students?

steps.prompt: Practice introducing yourself and your friends!

prompts.prompt: Say: He is my new friend.

questions.question_text: What does 'new' mean?
He ___ my friend.
They ___ new students.
Read: 'Ben and Kim are friends. They are new.' Who is new?
True or False: 'They' is used for two or more people.

steps.instruction: Let's sing and learn about letters! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Which letter comes after A in the alphabet?
questions.hint: It comes right after A, like 1, 2, 3!

questions.question_text: What sound does the letter A make in 'apple'?
questions.hint: Say 'apple' - what sound do you hear at the start?

questions.question_text: How many letters are in the alphabet?
questions.hint: We sing all the letters from A to Z. There are quite a lot!

items.definition: A set of letters
Say letters in order
A symbol in words
A group of letters

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 🔤

steps.title: Learning Letters
steps.text: Emma learns the alphabet at school. She points to each letter. The teacher helps her spell her name. Emma writes the word 'cat' in her book. She is happy!
steps.scene_description: young girl writing letters in notebook at classroom desk with teacher helping

questions.question: Where does Emma learn the alphabet?
What word does Emma write?
Who helps Emma spell her name?

steps.explanation: Letters have an order in the alphabet, from A to Z.
examples: The letter A comes before B.
The letter M comes before N.
The letter X comes before Y.

steps.audio_text: Good morning, class! Today we're learning the alphabet. Let's practice spelling. Can you spell the word 'cat'? Yes! C-A-T. Now let's say all the letters from A to Z together.

questions.question: What word does the teacher ask the children to spell?
What are the children learning today?
How does the teacher spell 'cat'?

steps.prompt: Practice speaking! Listen and repeat these sentences.

prompts.prompt: Say: He spells the word cat.

questions.question_text: Which word means 'to write letters in order to make a word'?
She ___ the alphabet every day.
Read: 'He knows all the letters from A to Z.' What does he know?
True or False: The alphabet has letters in it.
___ is spelling her name. Who is spelling?

steps.instruction: Let's review! Answer these questions about saying hello!

questions.question_text: When you meet someone for the first time, what do you say?
questions.hint: We use this word when we meet people and start talking to them.

questions.question_text: What do you say when you want to know someone's name?
questions.hint: This question helps you learn what someone is called.

questions.question_text: When your teacher says 'Good morning!', what can you say back?
questions.hint: You can say the same greeting back to your teacher!

items.definition: A friendly word we say when we meet someone
What you do with your ears to hear sounds
What you say when someone asks you a question
What people call you
Something you just got or just started
Say the letters of a word in order
One part of the alphabet like A, B, or C
A group of letters that means something
A short, friendly greeting
A word for two or more people or things
Something from a long time ago, not new
All the letters from A to Z
What you give when someone asks a question
What your family and friends call you
To say each letter of a word
To use your ears carefully

items.question: When you meet a friend, what do you say?
What do you use to hear your teacher?
What word means more than one person?
What do you call the letters A, B, C, D... all together?
A toy you just got from the store is...
When you say H-E-L-L-O, you... the word
What is your mom and dad's special word for you?

steps.title: At School
steps.text: Hello! My name is Sam. She is my new teacher. Her name is Ms. Lee. They are my old friends, Tom and Lily. We listen and answer questions together.
steps.scene_description: diverse children and teacher in bright elementary classroom, students raising hands

questions.question: Who is Ms. Lee?
Are Tom and Lily new friends?
What do the children do together?
options: They say hello to new people.

steps.explanation: We use 'he' for one boy, 'she' for one girl, and 'they' for two or more people.

items.explanation: After 'can' use the base verb without 's'
Use 'He' as subject and 'says' with he

steps.audio_text: Hello everyone! My name is Teacher Emma. Today we have two new students. They are Ben and Lucy. Let's all say hello to them. Now, can everyone listen carefully? I will spell a word from the alphabet. The word is H-E-L-L-O. Great job!

questions.question: How many new students are there?
What are the new students' names?
What word does the teacher spell?

steps.prompt: Let's practice speaking! Say these sentences about people.

prompts.prompt: Say: She can spell her name.
Say: They are new students and they listen to the teacher.
prompts.expected_text: They are new students and they listen to the teacher.

questions.question_text: What do you do when your teacher asks a question?
___ are new students in our class.
When you say the letters H-E-L-L-O, you ___ the word.
True or False: 'She' is used for one girl.
The alphabet has all the ___ from A to Z.
